summary: Three candidate edge sources tested with train/frozen-test discipline. All three negative. Nothing shipped into the flagging pipeline.

tests.name: Drift mechanism replication in Draw-No-Bet market
tests.question: The crowd-side-drifts-out edge works in 1X2. Does it replicate in an adjacent market on the same games?
tests.data: 775 finished football games, 238 with Draw-No-Bet opening+closing prices
tests.verdict: INCONCLUSIVE — samples far too small and the test half is flat. Not adopted.
tests.note: DNB is also near-duplicative of the 1X2 bet, so a positive here would have added little independent value.

tests.name: Both-teams-to-score crowd votes
tests.question: Sofascore serves a SECOND crowd vote (bothTeamsToScoreVote) on a market nobody cross-references. Does it beat the BTTS price?
tests.data: 234 games with both BTTS odds and BTTS votes (votes present on all 775 games)
tests.verdict: NEGATIVE — blending crowd votes made out-of-sample log loss WORSE than market-only.
tests.why: The crowd is Yes-biased on essentially every game (all 228 games sat in the >0.15 vote-minus-market bucket) while the market is well calibrated: 52.7% predicted vs 52.2% actual. The vote encodes a taste for goals, not information.

tests.name: Football Elo ratings model (src/ratings.js)
tests.question: Can a results-based ratings model add information the market lacks? This would be an edge independent of crowd sentiment and market movement.
tests.data: 54,625 finished football matches (2009-2026), walk-forward: ratings built only from matches finishing before each prediction. 1,631 panel games predicted.
tests.verdict: NEGATIVE — the train-set improvement did not replicate. The blend is worse than the market alone out-of-sample.
tests.bugFoundAndFixed: A unit test caught marginMultiplier(0) returning 0, which meant draws produced zero rating change — discarding ~25% of football results. Fixed (floored at 1) and the backtest re-run: the negative verdict held, so the conclusion is not an artifact of that bug.
tests.likelyCause: Team histories come from /events/last/0 (~30 recent matches each), so many teams are sparse, and matches from leagues of very different strength are pooled without a league-strength prior. A stronger version would need per-league baselines and deeper history.
tests.codeKept: src/ratings.js retained and unit-tested as a foundation, but deliberately NOT wired into flagging.

standing: drift-crowd (restricted to crowd-informative sports) remains the only component with positive out-of-sample evidence: frozen test +22.3% ROI over 94 bets.
